participants.reasoning: The extracted participants describe a much smaller single-center sample and different exclusions (off-pump CABG, deep hypothermic circulatory arrest, hemodialysis) than the official multicenter trial, which also included valve procedures and excluded active infection and renal replacement therapy.
participants.extracted: The study included adult patients aged 18–90 years scheduled for elective open heart cardiac surgery. Of 50 patients approached, 26 completed the trial: 14 assigned to the GIN (glucose and insulin administration while maintaining normoglycemia) group and 12 to the control group. Participants were similar at baseline with respect to age, sex, weight, height, comorbidity burden, diabetes status, HbA1c, creatinine, hematocrit, and ejection fraction. Patients scheduled for off-pump CABG, deep hypothermic circulatory arrest, elevated baseline troponin I (>0.5 ng/L), or hemodialysis were excluded.
participants.official:
Inclusion criteria: adults between 18 and 90 years old scheduled for elective coronary artery bypass grafting, valve repair or replacement, or a combination of these procedures with cardiopulmonary bypass between August 2007 and April 2015
Exclusion criteria: off‐pump cardiac surgery, anticipated hypothermic circulatory arrest, elevated baseline cardiac troponin I (greater than 0.5 ng/ ml^–1 , Montreal) or troponin T (greater than 0.1 ng/ml^–1 , Cleveland), kidney disease requiring renal replace

interventions.reasoning: The extracted description matches the official intervention’s core elements: fixed high-dose insulin with concomitant variable glucose infusion titrated to maintain normoglycemia during surgery, despite minor differences in the reported glucose range and wording.
interventions.extracted: The intervention was intraoperative glucose and insulin administration while maintaining normoglycemia (GIN). In the GIN group, patients received a priming bolus of 2 U insulin followed by a continuous insulin infusion at 5 mU/kg/min; approximately 10 minutes after the infusion began, and once blood glucose was below 6.1 mmol/L, 20% dextrose was administered intravenously. Blood glucose was checked every 5 to 15 minutes in the operating room and the dextrose infusion rate was adjusted to keep glucose between 3.5 and 6.1 mmol/L, with insulin infusion adjustments based on measured glucose values. Postoperatively, both groups were managed with standard glycemic protocols targeting blood glucose 4 to 10 mmol/L.
interventions.official:
Intervention(s):hyperinsulinaemic normoglycaemia, a fixed high‐dose insulin and concomitant variable glucose infusion titrated to glucose concentrations of 80 mg/dL to 110 mg/dL
Comparator(s):standard glycaemic management, low‐dose insulin infusion targeting glucose greater than 150 mg/dL (the standard protocol includes a blood glucose goal of 70 mg/dL to 150 mg/dL; see appendix 1; it is a complex protocol where supplemental boluses are given if acute increases (greater than 30 mg/dL) occur)

outcomes.reasoning: The extracted outcomes focus on postoperative cognitive and psychosocial measures at multiple time points, whereas the official outcomes are entirely different clinical endpoints centered on 30-day major postoperative complications, atrial fibrillation, length of stay, and 1-year mortality.
outcomes.extracted: The trial planned to measure postoperative cognitive outcomes using four standardized neuropsychological tests at three time points: approximately 2 weeks before surgery (baseline), 2 months after surgery, and 7 months after surgery. Primary cognitive outcomes were verbal learning and memory on the Rey Auditory Verbal Learning Test (RAVLT), including immediate recall and delayed recall of a 15-word list across repeated trials. Secondary cognitive outcomes included working memory on the Digit Span Task (forward and backward components), visuomotor tracking and attention on Trail Making Test A and B (number of errors and completion time in seconds), and implicit memory on the Word Pair Task (total number of correctly recalled word pairs, including associated and non-associated words, immediately and after a 20-minute delay). The study also administered questionnaires assessing self-esteem (Rosenberg Self-Esteem Inventory), depression (Beck Depression Index), chronic stress (Trier Inventory for the Assessment of Chronic Stress), and social support (Lubben Social Network Scale) at the study visits.
outcomes.official: Reported outcome(s) in full text of publication:the primary outcome was a collapsed composite (any vs none) of the following major postoperative complications occurring within 30 days of surgery: (1) all‐cause postoperative mortality; (2) failure to wean from cardiopulmonary bypass or postoperative low cardiac index (less than 1.8 l/min^–1 /m–2) requiring mechanical circulatory support with intra‐aortic balloon counter‐pulsation, ventricular assist device and/or extracorporeal mechanical oxygena
